prompt:
Our maintenance-notice emails are generated from big `@'...'@` here-string templates in the deploy scripts, and the current 'engine' is a chain of .Replace() calls that nobody trusts. I want a real one: `stencil.ps1` in the repo root, dot-sourceable. One hard rule from the repo lint gate: the engine is a MANUAL SCAN over the template text. `Invoke-Expression` (or its `iex` alias) and `$ExecutionContext` string expansion are banned — the test file scans your source and fails if they appear. Template text is data, never code.

`Expand-Stencil -Template <string> -Values <hashtable> [-OnMissing <Error|Empty|Keep>]`:

- Tokens look like `{{key}}` where key is one or more of `[A-Za-z0-9_.]`. Replace each token with the value from -Values, stringified ('' for $null — a key present with a $null value is NOT missing).
- Escaping: `{{{{` (scanning left to right) is a literal `{{` in the output and never starts a token. So '{{{{name}} and {{name}}' with name='x' comes out '{{name}} and x'. Lone braces and stray `}}` are just literal text.
- A `{{` that never closes: throw 'stencil: unclosed token at offset <n>' with the 0-based offset of that `{{`. A token whose key has characters outside the charset: throw 'stencil: invalid key: <key>'.
- Key lookup is CASE-SENSITIVE. Yes, `@{}` hashtables are case-insensitive — do not let that leak into template semantics: values @{name='x'} with template '{{Name}}' is a MISSING key.
- -OnMissing (default Error): Error throws 'stencil: missing key: <key>'; Empty substitutes ''; Keep leaves the token text exactly as written. Anything else for -OnMissing must error.
- ONE pass, left to right. A substituted value that itself contains '{{b}}' is literal output, never re-expanded.
- Empty template is fine and expands to ''.

`Get-StencilKeys -Template <string>`: the distinct keys referenced by the template, sorted ORDINALLY (not linguistically), as an array; empty when there are none. Escaped `{{{{` contributes nothing. Same unclosed/invalid-key errors as Expand-Stencil.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_stencil.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P2 item 3: here-string templating without Invoke-Expression/$ExecutionContext expansion (both source-scanned by the harness, incl. a word-boundary check for the iex alias), missing-key policies error/empty/keep, literal-brace escaping ({{{{ -> {{), plus single-pass no-reexpansion and exact offset/key error messages. PowerShell-native pins: hashtable case-INSENSITIVITY as the trap (@{name} must not satisfy {{Name}} — ContainsKey lies, forcing an ordinal scan over .Keys; no bash/py analog since their maps are case-sensitive), ValidateSet-or-equivalent on -OnMissing, ordinal key sort probed (B,_x,a,b). The real multiline template rides in as a fixture file authored from a literal @'...'@ here-string in the harness. Differentiation vs py-tmpl-compile/ts-tmpl-engine (nearest siblings): those build compile-to-render pipelines; this pins scan-level lexical semantics (escape sequence, offsets in error messages, single-pass) and the PS-specific eval ban. Domain (maintenance-notice templating) collides with nothing existing.
